_note: Pin weekly_snapshot AUDIT_LAYOUT names to ShipHero SKUs when fuzzy name matching can't bridge the gap (audit-layout 'Hair Clay' vs ShipHero 'Clay'; legacy V1 SKU mis-flagged is_kit=True hiding the real V2 single; packaging components like BW-SNTL-PK001 outranking the real product on on_hand; etc.). Keys are AUDIT_LAYOUT names (case-sensitive). Each entry pins to either a single 'sku' (one trusted physical SKU) or a list of 'skus' (sum on_hand across multiple physical SKUs that the audit layout treats as one product, e.g. 50ml + 100ml size variants, or scent variants under a single product page).
_methodology: Cross-reference against Shopify (source of truth) before adding/editing an entry. For multi-variant products (Body Wash, Body Lotion, Deodorant, Tallow Moisturizer), the 'skus' list should be the SKU of every Shopify variant tagged as a single/just-one variant under that product page. Use scripts/probe_shopify_canonical_skus.py to print the current Shopify mapping.
_format: Each entry: <AUDIT_LAYOUT name> -> {sku: <ShipHero SKU>} | {skus: [<ShipHero SKU>, ...]}. Edit when ShipHero canonical names drift, new size/scent variants ship, or Shopify reorganizes a product.
